text: David again gathered up all the chosen men of Israel, thirty thousand strong.
He and his whole army set out for Baale-judah to bring back the ark of God, called by the name of the LORD who sits enthroned above the cherubim.
They loaded the ark onto a new cart and carried it out of Abinadab's house on the hill; his sons Uzzah and Ahio guided the cart.
They brought it out with the ark of God on board, and Ahio walked in front of it.
David and the whole house of Israel celebrated with all their strength, singing and playing lyres, harps, tambourines, rattles, and cymbals.
When they reached Nacon's threshing floor, the oxen stumbled and Uzzah reached out and grabbed the ark to steady it.
The LORD's anger flared against Uzzah for that act, and he struck him down right there beside the ark.
David was upset that the LORD had broken out against Uzzah, and that spot has been called Perez-uzzah ever since.
David grew afraid of the LORD that day and asked how the ark could ever come to him now.
He decided not to bring the ark into his own city, and instead turned it aside to the house of Obed-edom of Gath.
The ark stayed in Obed-edom's house three months, and the LORD blessed him and his whole family because of it.
When David heard how the LORD had blessed Obed-edom's household on the ark's account, he went and joyfully brought the ark up into the City of David.
Every six steps the ark's bearers took, David stopped to sacrifice an ox and a fattened calf.
Wearing only a linen ephod, David danced before the LORD with everything he had.
So David and all Israel brought the ark up with shouts of joy and blasts of the horn.
As the ark entered the city, Michal watched from a window, and seeing King David leaping and whirling before the LORD, she looked down on him for it.
They set the ark inside the tent David had put up for it, and David offered burnt offerings and fellowship offerings before the LORD.
When the offerings were finished, David blessed the people in the name of the LORD of hosts.
He gave out a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a raisin cake to every person in the crowd, and then everyone went home.
When David returned to bless his own household, Michal met him and mocked him for exposing himself in front of servant girls like some shameless commoner.
David told her it was done before the LORD, who picked him over her father's whole house to lead Israel, and that he would keep celebrating before the LORD.
He added that he would make himself look even less dignified than that, but that the servant girls she mentioned would think highly of him for it.
Michal never had a child for the rest of her life.
